value_chain_position: Data-center power and thermal management infrastructure — sells power conversion systems, UPS, precision cooling, and liquid cooling solutions that every AI/HPC data center must install; a genuine chokepoint in the AI build-out value chain.
relative_comps: VRT is the closest thing to a chokepoint in AI data-center power and cooling infrastructure — its 29% YoY and 22% 3-year revenue CAGR on $10.8B TTM, with 21% FCF margin, reflect hyperscaler-capex pulling rather than selling. It competes with Eaton (ETN) and Schneider Electric (SU.PA) but has the highest AI-specific exposure mix (~70% of revenue tied to data-center deployments). Unlike ETN which has broader industrial diversification, VRT is the purest-play. The dual-theme tag (ai_infrastructure + energy_transition) is warranted per taxonomy notes: data-center power sits at the intersection of both. At ~$111B mcap, the valuation is demanding but the backlog visibility is multi-year. Primary risk is customer concentration (hyperscaler capex cycles).
